description: Trigger discrimination for negotiating specific cross-team DEPENDENCIES (this skill) vs turning a roadmap into commitments broadly (roadmap-to-commitments-translator), the integration BOUNDARY (architecture-designer), and the interface CONTRACT (api-event-architect).

cases.prompt: We depend on two other teams' deliverables. Surface those dependencies, negotiate concrete commitments with dates and owners, and set up escalation if they slip.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — mapping, committing, de-risking, and escalating specific cross-team dependencies is this skill. Not a whole roadmap and not the technical boundary.
cases.reason: The org/human negotiation of specific dependencies is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Turn our whole roadmap into what we can actually commit to this quarter, with confidence and dates.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — converting a roadmap into delivery commitments broadly is roadmap-to-commitments-translator; cross-team deps are one input to it.
cases.reason: roadmap-to-commitments-translator handles the whole roadmap→commitment translation. This skill negotiates the specific cross-team dependencies within an effort.

cases.prompt: Decide where the boundary between our two systems should be and who owns which component.
cases.reason: architecture-designer decides the technical integration boundary and ownership. This skill negotiates delivery across whatever boundary exists.

cases.prompt: Design the contract at the boundary: routes, envelope, versioning, and rate limits.
cases.reason: api-event-architect designs the interface contract. This skill negotiates who delivers it and when, against that contract.
